message:
fix(core): eliminate TLS isolation bug, condvar TOCTOU race, and allocator overflow

Three memory safety improvements in core threading and allocation:

1. TLS isolation (pthread/tls.rs): Replace static MAIN_TLS_VALUES with
   std::thread_local! fallback so unregistered threads get their own
   isolated storage instead of sharing state with the main thread.
   Fixes correctness for host-created threads that bypass pthread_create.

2. Condvar race (pthread/cond.rs): Use compare_exchange (AcqRel/Acquire)
   instead of load-then-store for mutex association in condvars. The
   previous pattern had a TOCTOU window where two threads could
   simultaneously associate different mutexes with the same condvar.

3. Allocator overflow (malloc/large.rs): Replace unchecked addition with
   checked_add() for next_base tracking and saturating_add() for
   total_mapped accumulation. Prevents integer overflow in allocation
   bookkeeping under extreme memory pressure.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
